top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, leaving accounts vulnerable to widespread takeover. The flaw affected key applications including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, enabling attackers to steal login credentials and data.

top_stories.title: Hackers used Meta’s AI support system to hijack over 20,000 Instagram accounts
top_stories.summary: Attackers hijacked 20,225 Instagram accounts by exploiting a vulnerability in Meta's AI-assisted account recovery system called High Touch Support (HTS). The flaw allowed unauthorized parties to perform password resets on Instagram accounts, representing a significant authentication and account recovery security failure.

top_stories.title: Qilin ransomware affiliate exploited Check Point VPN zero-day (CVE-2026-50751)
top_stories.summary: A Qilin ransomware affiliate is actively exploiting CVE-2026-50751, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Check Point VPN Remote Access and Mobile Access solutions. The zero-day affects Check Point Security Gateways and allows attackers to bypass authentication controls on both Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access products.

top_stories.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
top_stories.summary: Check Point disclosed a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-50751, CVSS 9.3) in its Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access products that is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw is a logic error in certificate validation affecting IKEv1 configurations, allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass password authentication.

top_stories.title: Building secure B2C applications with fine-grained access control using Amazon Cognito and Amazon Verified Permissions
top_stories.summary: AWS published a technical guide on building secure B2C applications using Amazon Cognito for authentication and Amazon Verified Permissions for fine-grained authorization. The article covers implementing authentication and authorization controls together as core security pillars for modern web applications.

news.title: Treating AI agents like service accounts for federated query security
news.summary: Starburst CISO Paras Malhotra explains how the company manages access controls and data governance across federated query environments, including how AI agents are treated like service accounts with associated audit trails. The discussion covers layering access controls above native source permissions, vendor risk tiering across 200+ partners, and managing autonomous agent access through MCP protocols.

news.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
news.summary: Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group attributed a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign targeting U.S. organizations in professional, legal, and financial services to threat actor UNC3753, active between January and May 2026. The campaign employed vishing (voice phishing) and physical intrusions as attack vectors to gain unauthorized access and steal data.

news.title: AI Agent Governance: Managing Permissions, Auditing, and Compliance
news.summary: The article covers AI agent governance in enterprise .NET applications, focusing on permissions, role-based access management, auditing, and compliance controls. It addresses how organizations can manage and monitor access for AI agents, an emerging topic intersecting traditional IAM concepts with agentic AI systems.

news.title: SingleID、Microsoft Entra IDとのSCIM連携を提供開始
news.summary: SingleID has launched SCIM integration with Microsoft Entra ID. This integration enables automated user provisioning and deprovisioning between SingleID and Microsoft Entra ID.

news.title: Willow raises $7M to build the identity layer for autonomous AI agents
news.summary: Willow has raised $7M to develop an identity layer specifically designed for autonomous AI agents. The funding targets a emerging IAM challenge around non-human identities and agentic AI systems operating within enterprise environments.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server now supports cross-account and cross-role access
news.summary: AWS has added cross-account and cross-role access functionality to its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server within the Agent Toolkit for AWS. The feature enables developers using AI coding agents to work across multiple AWS accounts and roles without manual credential switching.

news.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect in ASP.NET Core
news.summary: An article provides guidance on implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols within ASP.NET Core applications. The content covers authentication, authorization, token management, and Single Sign-On configuration for developers building identity-aware applications.

news.title: Pink is the latest goon squad to use fake helpdesk calls to steal creds
news.summary: A cybercriminal group called Pink is using fake helpdesk calls to steal credentials, employing social engineering tactics similar to those previously used by the Lapsus$ group. The attack method involves impersonating IT helpdesk personnel to trick targets into divulging login credentials.

news.title: Customize federated sign-in with new Amazon Cognito Lambda trigger
news.summary: Amazon Cognito has introduced a new Lambda trigger that allows developers to customize federated sign-in flows in user pools. The feature enables customization of authentication behavior for users signing in via external identity providers within Cognito's managed access service.

news.title: Gartner: Cyber Leaders Must Treat Identity as Infrastructure as AI Attack Surfaces Multiply
news.summary: Gartner has issued guidance stating that cyber leaders must treat identity as core infrastructure in response to expanding AI-driven attack surfaces. The advisory emphasizes modernizing identity systems and measuring resilience as part of addressing four key threat areas where attackers currently hold an advantage.

news.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
news.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded to target AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing techniques to compromise identity and authentication systems.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to suggest potential criminal charges. The dispute centers on Microsoft's response to unsolicited vulnerability disclosures and the legal threats directed at the researcher.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass account security controls and reset Instagram passwords, successfully compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant profiles. The attack method involved social engineering instructions circulated on Telegram that manipulated the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets, representing an authentication bypass via AI-assisted support tooling.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets on a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. CISA is working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials while facing congressional inquiries from both chambers.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes along with credentials to multiple privileged accounts.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.
